source: SARS Rates of Tax for Individuals — Budget Speech 25 February 2026 (Minister Enoch Godongwana)

retirement_fund_deduction.note: Deduction = greater of 27.5% of taxable income OR remuneration, capped at R430,000/year (up from R350,000). Effective 1 March 2026 per Budget Speech.

notes.calculation_method: Annual equivalent method — annualise periodic taxable income, apply brackets, subtract rebates, floor at 0, de-annualise to periodic PAYE
notes.weekly_conversion: Weekly payroll: multiply weekly taxable income by 52, apply annual brackets, subtract annual rebates, divide result by 52
notes.rounding: Annual tax: round to nearest rand (AwayFromZero). Period PAYE: round to nearest cent.
notes.ty2027_changes: Budget Speech 25 Feb 2026: all 7 brackets inflation-adjusted ~3.4%; primary rebate R17,235→R17,820; secondary R9,444→R9,765; tertiary R3,145→R3,249; thresholds below-65 R95,750→R99,000; 65-74 R148,217→R153,250; 75+ R165,689→R171,300; retirement fund cap R350,000→R430,000
notes.vs_ty2026: TY2026 rule set document: SARS_PAYE_2026 (expired 28 Feb 2026). This document supersedes it for pay periods from 1 March 2026.
